subject: Counting and Combinatorics
question: How many numbers in the range 1000 - 9999 have no repeated digits?
explanation: A 4-step process: (1) Choose first digit, (2) choose second digit, (3) choose third digit, (4) choose fourth digit. Every step can be done in a number of ways that does not depend on previous choices, and each number can be specified in this manner. So there are $9 \cdot 9 \cdot 8 \cdot 7 = 4{,}536$ ways
